chapter_context.summary: Contrasts the wicked fleeing when no man pursues with the bold righteous, highlights the blessing of confessing sins, and warns against hasty wealth.

outline.text: Wicked Fleeing, understanding, Robbing Poor, and Keeping Law
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Wicked Fleeing, understanding, Robbing Poor, and Keeping Law (28:1-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: Usury Increase, Confessing and Forsaking Sin, and Hard Hearts
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Usury Increase, Confessing and Forsaking Sin, and Hard Hearts (28:8-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: Wicked Ruler, Unjust Gain, Hasty Riches, and Evil Eye
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Wicked Ruler, Unjust Gain, Hasty Riches, and Evil Eye (28:15-22), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: Rebuking Men, Despising Parents, and Giving to Poor
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Rebuking Men, Despising Parents, and Giving to Poor (28:23-28), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

key_themes.title: Wisdom vs. Folly
key_themes.description: Proverbs outlines a practical blueprint for daily living, contrasting the actions, words, and consequences of the wise with those of the foolish.

key_themes.title: The Fear of the Lord
key_themes.description: The foundational principle of wisdom is a reverent awe and trust in God, which guides moral choices and shapes character.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the practical sayings of Chapter 28, what is one specific area of your speech, relationships, or work where you can apply divine wisdom today?
How does this chapter prompt you to choose the path of integrity and diligence over convenience or compromise?

cultural_historical.title: Ancient Near Eastern Wisdom Literature
cultural_historical.text: Proverbs shares structural and thematic similarities with other ancient wisdom writings, but stands unique in placing the Fear of Yahweh as the foundation of all true wisdom.
